title: Per-project playbook activation preferences (RFC-011 R1)
description: Schema for <project>/.episodic-memory/playbooks.json — an OPTIONAL, operator-authored, per-project declaration of which playbook lesson episodes load at session start or on demand. Selection is by EPISODE ID ONLY (any chain member; the build resolves to the terminal active revision, RFC-011 R2). Schema-validated, bounded (at most 32 entries, 64 KiB), failure-contained: a malformed/schema-invalid/over-bound file is skipped with a build-report note and a stderr line, never fatal, and degrades to no playbooks loaded (RFC-011 R1). The shared parser parsePlaybooksConfig (scripts/em-trigger-index.mjs, §12) is the single source of truth for build + protection; the build (R2) fails open (skip+note), the retention consumers (R5b) fail closed (abort exit 1).
$comment: Two bounds CANNOT be expressed in the closed-subset instance validator (scripts/lib/json-instance-validate.mjs) and are enforced by parsePlaybooksConfig instead, keeping this schema as the authoritative contract: (1) the 64 KiB FILE-SIZE bound is a stat (a size, not a JSON-Schema constraint); (2) 'no two entries share the same id' is unique-BY-id, which uniqueItems cannot express for object arrays (it checks whole-item deep equality, so two entries with the same id but different mode would slip through). R1 states both as parser-enforced. validateInstance models exactly the keywords used here (type/const/enum/minLength/maxItems/additionalProperties/required/properties/items/if/then/not/$ref/$defs) so the parser validates against THIS schema as the single source of truth.

schema_version.description: Contract version (matches the derived-index convention). const 1 (RFC-011 R1).

playbooks.description: At most 32 entries (Principle 6 bound). The 64 KiB whole-FILE bound is enforced by parsePlaybooksConfig as a stat.

bounds.description: Optional render-cap bounds. max_playbooks caps the session_start.playbooks derived array at BUILD time (R2); it never gates at event time.

max_playbooks.description: Schema-validated integer 1..4 (out-of-range rejected, NEVER clamped — 0 and 5 fail). Absent -> build default 2 (R1).

playbookEntry.description: One playbook declaration. `id` may be ANY member of a supersedes chain; the build resolves to the terminal active revision (R2). `mode` is a closed enum. `triggers` (optional, on_demand ONLY) uses the RFC-009 R1 trigger grammar; a declared triggers array REPLACES the episode's own trigger set within this project (override, not addition).

id.description: An episode id (any chain member). Tag-based selectors are REJECTED by design (RFC-009 R10).

mode.description: session_start: surface at every session start. on_demand: surface when the RFC-009 R3 matcher fires.

triggers.description: Optional, on_demand ONLY. RFC-009 R1 trigger grammar (phrase, tool:, activity:). Override: when present it replaces the episode's own trigger set entirely (an empty/whitespace-only set yields an empty effective trigger set, excluded at build as empty_triggers — R2.2).

then.description: triggers is rejected on a session_start entry (it is on_demand-only).
